name: 1988 Mass Executions of Political Prisoners
description: In late July 1988, [Ruhollah Khomeini](/people/ruhollah-khomeini) issued a secret fatwa ordering the execution of political prisoners across Iran who remained loyal to the Mojahedin-e Khalq (MEK/PMOI) or refused to renounce their political beliefs. Three-person 'Death Commissions' were established in prisons throughout Iran, each comprising a Sharia judge, a prosecutor, and an intelligence representative. In Tehran, the commission — chaired by [Hossein-Ali Nayyeri](/people/hossein-ali-nayyeri), with [Morteza Eshraqi](/people/morteza-eshraqi) as prosecutor, [Ebrahim Raisi](/people/ebrahim-raisi) as deputy prosecutor, and [Mostafa Pourmohammadi](/people/mostafa-pourmohammadi) as Intelligence Ministry representative — processed prisoners in minutes. Estimated deaths range from 2,800 to 5,000 (UN, HRW, Amnesty) or higher. The victims were buried in secret mass graves. Ayatollah Montazeri, then designated successor to Khomeini, protested and was subsequently dismissed as heir. In 2016 his family released an audio recording of his 15 August 1988 meeting with Death Committee members in which he said: 'The greatest crime committed during the reign of the Islamic Republic... has been committed by you.' The UN Special Rapporteur on Iran concluded in a 2024 report that the executions constituted crimes against humanity and genocide. None of the perpetrators has faced legal accountability.
editorial_notes: Fatwa estimated issued 28 July 1988. Executions ran through approximately November 1988. Montazeri audio published August 2016. UN 2024 report: classified as crimes against humanity and genocide.
